utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: yeah so sorry so barbara is a 31 -year-old female and she has history of diabetes type two and asthma and she is here today with abdominal pain so barbara tell me a little more about your abdominal pain what's been going on

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: so i've been having this pain for the past one week and as you know i have a longstanding history of diabetes and i guess i've been drinking too much coffee lately i do n't know if that triggered my gastritis so

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: yeah

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i think it's been but lately

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay alright and so is it increasing in severity would you say or staying about the same or what

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: it's gradually increasing for sure

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay alright and along with the pain have you had any nausea or vomiting or diarrhea

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: nausea and vomiting is on and off

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hmmm

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: so i would say i've experienced nausea for about like three times in the past week

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay okay and and so and you vomited how many times

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: a couple of times

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay alright been drinking a lot of coffee you said too uh is that is that starbucks i love those nitrocolbrus and starbucks we ever had those those are incredible

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: that's

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: and the venti

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: yeah the for sure you got ta go venti on that you really have to coax them into it though they wo n't even give you it's it's like you need a license to get a venti on that that nitro it's so strong so that's my achilles heel i tell you but okay so you think that might have you think maybe coffee contributed to your abdominal pain uh you you you you've been drinking more coffee and maybe that's related as you're thinking

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah i think so because i started last week and towards the end of last week my coffee intake was pretty bad so i've been controlling myself this week and you know i i probably just had one cup

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay yeah okay and so is the pain in your upper abdomen or where in your abdomen is it

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: it's on both the sides of my upper abdomen

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hmmm okay

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: so i it also increases when i work out like if i wan na jog or like go for a run my abdominal pain just like spikes you know so i have to take it easy

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay alright understood you've not had any fever with that pain have you

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no i have n't

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay alright and so how about your diabetes how's your diabetes been doing how your blood sugars been what low one hundreds two hundreds where where is it

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i think it's been under control it's eight hundred so

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay good and you're still taking the metformin five hundred milligrams once a day right no actually it looks like last visit we increased that to five hundred milligrams twice per day is that correct

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yes that's probably helping me keeping it under control so

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay good good and then for your asthma have you had any recent asthma attacks and are you still taking the flovent twice a day and albuterol as needed for wheezing

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yes but sometimes i forget you know i have n't had any asthma attacks lately so i just figured i could decrease

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: yep okay

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i think that's a good decision though

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: yeah well better to stay on the flovent daily and then just use the albuterol if you need it but if you stay on the flovent daily it will decrease the frequency of those attacks so yeah alright well let's go ahead and examine you okay so on your physical exam it's pretty normal for the most part unremarkable but i i'll i'll say on your on your heart exam you do still have a grade three out of six systolic ejection murmur that's unchanged from your prior exam and and i'm not worried about that we're kinda watching that but it just means i hear some heart sounds when your valves are moving and otherwise normal heart exam for your abdominal exam you do have mild tenderness in the epigastrium of your abdomen your abdomen's otherwise soft there's no tenderness in the right lower quadrant whatsoever and there is no significant tenderness in the right upper quadrant so i think you know that's right where your stomach is in the upper abdomen so i think that you know that may be you may be right this could be some gastritis so so then otherwise on your exam on your lung exam you do have some mild end expiratory wheezes very faint and otherwise normal lung exam with excellent air movement and otherwise pretty normal physical exam so let's talk about your my assessment and your plan here so first of all for the first problem of the abdominal pain i do think that you have acute gastritis and i think this is probably related to the caffeine intake and i i know that you've had a a moderately heavy history of alcohol use too so i want you to cut back on the alcohol as well you know keep that down to a dull roller like maybe keep that less than two or three drinks a week would be great and then also cut back on the caffeine and that should help and i'm going to write you a prescription for zantac as well as we need and so i'll write you a prescription you can take that twice a day that should help with the acid in your stomach and the pain and then i'm going to check a urinalysis a urine pregnancy test a cbc a comprehensive metabolic panel as well and we'll see what those results show just to be sure it's nothing else going on and then for your second problem of diabetes type two let's continue you on the metformin but i wan na adjust the dose slightly i'm gon na increase the morning dose to one thousand milligrams and the evening dose we can keep at five hundred so we'll go metformin one thousand milligrams in the morning and five hundred milligrams in the evening please continue to check your blood sugars let me know what they are when you come back you know we just keep track of those and then we'll see you back in four weeks by the way i wan na have you follow up with me in four weeks and for your third problem of asthma let's continue you on the flovent that seems to be doing pretty well continue to take that daily and then also albuterol as needed so how does that sound for a plan any other questions comments suggestions

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: that sounds good and i also feel like i'm eating been eating a lot of spicy food

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hmmm

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: lately that could be contributing towards my gastritis so i'm gon na cut back on that as well

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay yeah i'd also recommend while this is hurting you definitely cut back on the spices the spicy food easier said than done sometimes i know but yep yep alright sounds good well listen i'll see you back in four weeks give me a holler if if you have any questions or anything else in the meantime

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i will

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: alright you take care sure you got

detail_list.summary: The patient is a 31-year-old female with a history of diabetes type 2 and asthma presenting with abdominal pain.
Abdominal pain
The patient reports abdominal pain for one week and suspects increased coffee intake triggered gastritis.
The doctor asks if the abdominal pain is increasing in severity.
The patient reports the abdominal pain is gradually increasing.
The doctor asks if the patient has experienced nausea, vomiting, or diarrhea.
The patient reports intermittent nausea and vomiting.
The patient reports experiencing nausea three times in the past week.
The doctor asks how many times the patient has vomited.
The patient reports vomiting a couple of times.
The doctor asks for the location of the abdominal pain.
The patient reports pain on both sides of the upper abdomen.
The patient reports that abdominal pain spikes during physical activity like jogging.
The doctor asks if the patient has had a fever.
The patient denies having a fever.
Diabetes
The doctor asks about the patient's blood sugar levels.
The patient reports blood sugar is under control.
The doctor asks if the patient is taking metformin 500mg twice daily.
The patient confirms taking metformin 500mg twice daily.
Asthma
The doctor asks about recent asthma attacks and adherence to Flovent and albuterol.
The patient reports no recent asthma attacks and admits to occasionally forgetting to take Flovent.
The doctor advises the patient to continue daily Flovent and as-needed albuterol.
Physical exam findings: Grade 3/6 systolic ejection murmur, mild epigastric tenderness, soft abdomen, no RLQ/RUQ tenderness, mild end-expiratory wheezes.
Assessment: Acute gastritis likely related to caffeine and alcohol.
The doctor advises reducing alcohol and caffeine intake, prescribes Zantac twice daily, and orders urinalysis, urine pregnancy test, CBC, and CMP.
The doctor adjusts metformin to 1000mg in the morning and 500mg in the evening.
The patient is to follow up in 4 weeks.
The patient reports high intake of spicy food.
The patient agrees to cut back on spicy food.
The doctor reinforces the recommendation to reduce spicy food intake.
